attacking_style: Pure sprint finisher who excels in messy, disrupted situations requiring improvisation. Demonstrates ability to win from compromised positions rather than textbook leadouts, particularly effective in tight, technical finales.
team_tactics: Evolved from domestique/leadout role to protected sprinter (2020-2023). Wins often come when traditional leadout trains fail or teammates absent, suggesting reliance on individual positioning over organized trains. DSM showed confused hierarchy in earlier races.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints with technical corners where bike handling compensates for positioning. Exposed on steep gradients and final climbs, suggesting weight/power-to-weight disadvantage limits him to pure flat stages.
vulnerability: Positioning in predictable, high-speed finales (caught 50th in criterium). Distanced on steep climbs. Most success comes when chaos disrupts stronger leadout teams, suggesting pure power deficit against top-tier sprinters in organized sprints.
key_patterns: Wins from disrupted sprints: crash at 2.3km (Vuelta), teammate illness (Giro), compromised position (UAE). 3+ instances.
Career trajectory: domestique to leadout to protected sprinter (2020-2023), multiple signals of miscast role.
Superior bike handling in technical finales: tight corners, messy situations. Confirmed across 3 races.
Consistent weakness on climbs/gradients despite pure sprinter classification. Noted in 2 separate observations.
Beats top sprinters (Gaviria, Ewan, Cavendish, Ganna) when leadout advantage neutralized by race circumstances.
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta win showed tactical maturity, self-sufficiency without team support. Evolution from opportunistic wins to adaptable, complete sprinter confirmed over 4-year span.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 19
